name: Rippo v. Baker
question: Did a trial judge violate the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment when he failed to recuse himself from a criminal case where the district attorney’s office prosecuting the case was simultaneously investigating him on allegations of bribery?
description: A case in which the Court determined that the proper standard of review for judicial recusal is an objective inquiry that asks whether the probability of actual bias on the part of the judge or decisionmaker is too high to be constitutionally tolerable.
